Apple___Apple_scab.cause: Fungal disease favored by cool, wet weather.
Apple___Apple_scab.symptoms: Olive-green or dark spots on leaves and fruit, leaf curling, fruit cracking.
Apple___Apple_scab.treatment: Remove infected leaves/fruits and apply approved fungicide if infection is severe.
Apple___Apple_scab.prevention: Prune for airflow, remove fallen leaves, avoid overhead watering.

Apple___Black_rot.cause: Fungal infection affecting leaves, fruit, and branches.
Apple___Black_rot.symptoms: Dark circular fruit rot, leaf spots, branch cankers.
Apple___Black_rot.treatment: Remove infected fruit and branches; apply suitable fungicide.
Apple___Black_rot.prevention: Sanitize orchard, prune dead wood, destroy infected debris.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.cause: Fungal disease spreading between cedar/juniper and apple trees.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.symptoms: Orange-yellow spots on leaves, premature leaf drop.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.treatment: Remove nearby infected cedar galls if possible; apply fungicide early.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.prevention: Use resistant apple varieties and improve spacing.

Apple___healthy.prevention: Continue regular monitoring, pruning, and balanced nutrition.

Blueberry___healthy.prevention: Maintain soil acidity, irrigation, and regular inspection.

Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.cause: Fungal disease favored by humid conditions.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.symptoms: White powdery growth on leaves, distorted young leaves.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.treatment: Remove infected leaves and apply approved sulfur/fungicide spray.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.prevention: Improve airflow and avoid dense canopy growth.

Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.prevention: Maintain pruning, sanitation, and pest monitoring.

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.cause: Fungal disease favored by warm, humid weather.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.symptoms: Long rectangular gray or tan lesions on leaves.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.treatment: Use recommended fungicide when disease pressure is high.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.prevention: Crop rotation, residue management, resistant hybrids.

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.symptoms: Small orange-brown pustules on leaf surfaces.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.treatment: Apply suitable fungicide if severe; remove heavily infected leaves.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.prevention: Use resistant varieties and monitor during humid weather.

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.cause: Fungal disease favored by wet and moderate temperatures.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.symptoms: Long cigar-shaped gray-green lesions on leaves.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.treatment: Apply fungicide during early infection if needed.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.prevention: Use resistant hybrids, rotate crops, manage residue.

Corn_(maize)___healthy.prevention: Continue field monitoring and balanced fertilization.

Grape___Black_rot.cause: Fungal infection common in warm, humid conditions.
Grape___Black_rot.symptoms: Brown leaf spots, black shriveled berries.
Grape___Black_rot.treatment: Remove infected fruit and apply recommended fungicide.
Grape___Black_rot.prevention: Prune vines, improve airflow, remove mummified berries.

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).symptoms: Tiger-stripe leaf patterns, berry spotting, vine decline.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).treatment: Prune infected wood and remove severely affected vines.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).prevention: Avoid pruning wounds in wet weather and sanitize tools.

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.symptoms: Brown angular leaf spots and drying leaf tissue.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.treatment: Remove infected leaves and apply suitable fungicide.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.prevention: Improve canopy airflow and avoid overhead irrigation.

Grape___healthy.prevention: Maintain pruning, irrigation, and vineyard sanitation.

Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).cause: Bacterial disease spread by citrus psyllids.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).symptoms: Yellow mottled leaves, bitter small fruits, tree decline.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).treatment: Remove severely infected trees and control psyllid insects.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).prevention: Use disease-free planting material and monitor psyllids.

Peach___Bacterial_spot.cause: Bacterial infection favored by warm, wet conditions.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small dark leaf spots, shot-hole leaves, fruit lesions.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Remove infected parts and use approved copper-based sprays carefully.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use resistant varieties and avoid overhead irrigation.

Peach___healthy.prevention: Maintain orchard hygiene and regular monitoring.

Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.cause: Bacterial disease spread by splashing water and infected seed.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Water-soaked spots on leaves, dark lesions, leaf drop.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Remove infected plants and apply approved copper-based treatment.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use clean seeds, avoid overhead watering, rotate crops.

Pepper,_bell___healthy.prevention: Maintain crop hygiene and regular inspection.

Potato___Early_blight.cause: Fungal disease common in warm, humid conditions.
Potato___Early_blight.symptoms: Dark concentric ring spots on older leaves.
Potato___Early_blight.treatment: Remove infected leaves and apply suitable fungicide.
Potato___Early_blight.prevention: Crop rotation, avoid water stress, remove plant debris.

Potato___Late_blight.cause: Water mold pathogen favored by cool, wet weather.
Potato___Late_blight.symptoms: Dark water-soaked leaf lesions, white mold underside, rapid plant collapse.
Potato___Late_blight.treatment: Remove infected plants and apply recommended fungicide quickly.
Potato___Late_blight.prevention: Use certified seed, avoid overhead watering, monitor weather risk.

Potato___healthy.prevention: Continue monitoring and good field sanitation.

Raspberry___healthy.prevention: Maintain pruning, spacing, and regular monitoring.

Soybean___healthy.prevention: Monitor for pests, maintain soil fertility, rotate crops.

Squash___Powdery_mildew.cause: Fungal disease favored by warm, humid conditions.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.symptoms: White powdery patches on leaves and stems.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.treatment: Remove affected leaves and apply approved fungicide or sulfur spray.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.prevention: Improve airflow, avoid crowding, use resistant varieties.

Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.symptoms: Purple-red leaf spots, scorched leaf edges, reduced plant vigor.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.treatment: Remove infected leaves and apply recommended fungicide.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.prevention: Use clean plants, improve airflow, avoid wet foliage.

Strawberry___healthy.prevention: Maintain clean beds and avoid excess moisture.

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.cause: Bacterial infection spread by splashing water and infected seed.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small dark leaf spots, yellowing, fruit lesions.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Remove infected leaves and use approved copper-based treatment.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use disease-free seed, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops.

Tomato___Early_blight.cause: Fungal disease affecting older leaves first.
Tomato___Early_blight.symptoms: Brown spots with concentric rings, yellowing leaves.
Tomato___Early_blight.treatment: Remove infected leaves and apply suitable fungicide.
Tomato___Early_blight.prevention: Mulch soil, rotate crops, avoid leaf wetness.

Tomato___Late_blight.cause: Water mold pathogen favored by cool, wet weather.
Tomato___Late_blight.symptoms: Dark water-soaked leaf patches, white mold underside, rapid spread.
Tomato___Late_blight.treatment: Remove infected plants and apply recommended fungicide quickly.
Tomato___Late_blight.prevention: Avoid overhead watering, improve airflow, monitor humid weather.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.cause: Fungal disease common in humid greenhouse or dense canopy conditions.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.symptoms: Yellow patches on upper leaves, olive mold underside.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.treatment: Remove infected leaves and improve ventilation.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.prevention: Reduce humidity, increase spacing, avoid wet leaves.

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.cause: Fungal disease spread by rain splash and infected debris.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.symptoms: Small circular spots with gray centers and dark edges.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.treatment: Remove infected lower leaves and apply approved fungicide.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.prevention: Mulch soil, rotate crops, remove plant debris.

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.cause: Mite infestation, common in hot and dry conditions.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.symptoms: Tiny yellow speckles, webbing, leaf bronzing.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.treatment: Spray water to reduce mites and apply suitable miticide if severe.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.prevention: Avoid dusty dry conditions and monitor undersides of leaves.

Tomato___Target_Spot.cause: Fungal disease favored by warm, humid weather.
Tomato___Target_Spot.symptoms: Brown circular lesions with target-like rings.
Tomato___Target_Spot.treatment: Remove infected leaves and apply suitable fungicide.
Tomato___Target_Spot.prevention: Improve airflow, avoid overhead watering, rotate crops.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.cause: Viral disease spread mainly by whiteflies.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.symptoms: Yellow curled leaves, stunted growth, reduced fruiting.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.treatment: Remove infected plants and control whiteflies.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.prevention: Use resistant varieties, insect nets, and whitefly management.

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.cause: Viral disease spread through contact, tools, and infected material.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.symptoms: Mottled leaves, distorted growth, reduced fruit quality.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.treatment: Remove infected plants; disinfect tools and hands.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.prevention: Use virus-free seed, sanitize tools, avoid tobacco contamination.

Tomato___healthy.prevention: Continue regular watering, nutrition, and pest monitoring.
